description:
You are a container-platform specialist who has just received the nightly build of three internal micro-services.  
Their versioned payloads are already unpacked for you under

    /home/user/releases/

as the following directories:

    auth-service-3.4.1/
    data-service-2.1.0/
    gateway-service-1.8.7/

Each of those release directories already contains a single text file called “README.md”.

Your assignment is to make sure that the “current-release” tree always exposes a short, human-friendly
symlink for the **latest** version of every service, and that a machine-readable report of those links is kept.

1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/current-release  
   if it does not already exist (it must be a real directory, **not** a symlink).

2. Inside /home/user/current-release create, **or atomically replace if they already exist**, the following three symbolic links exactly as listed:
       auth    → /home/user/releases/auth-service-3.4.1
       data    → /home/user/releases/data-service-2.1.0
       gateway → /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.8.7

3. A new build of the gateway micro-service has just landed.  
   Manufacture the directory

       /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0

   and copy the file README.md from the previous gateway release so that the new directory also contains one file named README.md with identical contents.

4. Point the “gateway” symlink that you created in step 2 to this **new** directory so that it now resolves to  
       /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0

5. Create a plain-text status file called

       /home/user/link-report.txt

   with one line per symlink, **sorted alphabetically by the symlink’s file-name**.  
   Each line must follow this exact template (note the spaces):

       <link-name> -> <absolute-target-path>

   Therefore, after all changes, **the file must contain exactly these three lines and nothing else**:

       auth -> /home/user/releases/auth-service-3.4.1
       data -> /home/user/releases/data-service-2.1.0
       gateway -> /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0

   Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline.

6. Display the file’s contents to the console so that the CI system can capture and echo them.

Hints & constraints:
• Do everything under /home/user without sudo.  
• Replace existing links atomically (do not leave dangling links).  
• Only symbolic links should live inside /home/user/current-release; no regular copies of the directories are allowed there.  
• The agent is expected to solve the task in roughly 5-10 shell commands.
truth:
Initial state prepared for the container:

Directory tree that already exists before execution:
 /home/user/
 ├── releases/
 │   ├── auth-service-3.4.1/
 │   │   └── README.md (plain text, content = “Auth service build 3.4.1\n”)
 │   ├── data-service-2.1.0/
 │   │   └── README.md (plain text, content = “Data service build 2.1.0\n”)
 │   └── gateway-service-1.8.7/
 │       └── README.md (plain text, content = “Gateway service build 1.8.7\n”)

No /home/user/current-release directory or link exists yet.

Desired final state after agent work:

1. Directory: /home/user/current-release  (mode 755, type “directory”)

2. Symlinks inside it:
   • /home/user/current-release/auth    → /home/user/releases/auth-service-3.4.1
   • /home/user/current-release/data    → /home/user/releases/data-service-2.1.0
   • /home/user/current-release/gateway → /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0
   (all three must be of file type “symbolic link”, not regular files)

3. Newly created directory: /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0
   • contains one file README.md whose contents are byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.8.7/README.md   
     (i.e. “Gateway service build 1.8.7\n”)

4. Log file: /home/user/link-report.txt  
   Text contents (including final newline):

auth -> /home/user/releases/auth-service-3.4.1
data -> /home/user/releases/data-service-2.1.0
gateway -> /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0

5. Verification criteria used by the automated test:
   • The directory /home/user/current-release exists and is a directory.
   • The three symlinks exist, have the exact names and resolve to the exact target paths above.
   • /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0 exists and is a directory.
   • /home/user/releases/gateway-service-1.9.0/README.md exists and its content is exactly:
       “Gateway service build 1.8.7\n”
   • /home/user/link-report.txt exists, is a regular file, and its content matches precisely the three-line block above, terminated with a single newline.
